content:
# Exodus Chapter 29 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~50r–51v (PDF pp. 99–102); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 71–73 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_029_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_029_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_029_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_029_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`, `EXO_029_vaticanus_page_003.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE CONSECRATION OF THE PRIESTS:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 29 across two folio pages in standard prose column format with full Masoretic vocalization and cantillation. This chapter completes the transition begun in chapter 28 — from garments to ceremony, from priestly clothing to priestly consecration. The chapter moves through a carefully ordered ritual sequence: washing and vesting (vv. 1-9), the bull sin offering (vv. 10-14), the first ram as burnt offering (vv. 15-18), the ram of ordination with its unique blood ritual (vv. 19-28), the succession of garments (vv. 29-30), the sacred ordination meal (vv. 31-34), and the seven-day repetition (vv. 35-37). The chapter then transitions from ordination to daily worship — the perpetual tamid offerings (vv. 38-42) — and culminates in YHWH's solemn promise to dwell among His people (vv. 43-46). The Vaticanus manuscript (pp. 71-73) presents the LXX text across three pages, following the MT sequence closely with minor expansions.

---

## Washing and Vesting of the Priests (vv. 1–9)

**1** And this [ve-zeh] is the thing [ha-davar] which [asher] you shall do [ta'aseh] to them [lahem] to consecrate [le-qaddesh] them [otam] to be priests [le-khahen] to me [li]: take [qakh] one [par] young bull [ben-baqar ekhad] and rams [ve-eilim] — two [shnayim] without blemish [temimim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְזֶה הַדָּבָר אֲשֶׁר־תַּעֲשֶׂה לָהֶם לְקַדֵּשׁ אֹתָם לְכַהֵן לִי לְקַח פַּר אֶחָד בֶּן־בָּקָר וְאֵלִם שְׁנַיִם תְּמִימִם. **Key terms:** לְקַדֵּשׁ (le-qaddesh) = piel infinitive construct of קדשׁ (qadash, "to be holy") — "to consecrate, to make holy"; the entire chapter answers the question: how does a mortal become fit to stand before YHWH?

**2** And bread [ve-lekhem] of unleavened bread [matstsot] and cakes [ve-khallot] of unleavened bread [matstsot] mixed [belulot] with oil [ba-shamen] and wafers [u-reqiqei] of unleavened bread [matstsot] anointed [meshukhim] with oil [ba-shamen]; of wheat flour [sollet khittim] you shall make [ta'aseh] them [otam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלֶחֶם מַצּוֹת וְחַלֹּת מַצּוֹת בְּלוּלֹת בַּשֶּׁמֶן וּרְקִיקֵי מַצּוֹת מְשֻׁחִים בַּשָּׁמֶן סֹלֶת חִטִּים תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתָם. **Key terms:** מַצּוֹת (matstsot) = "unleavened bread" — from the root meaning "to squeeze out, drain"; bread without leaven, associated with haste and purity.

**3** And you shall put [ve-natattah] them [otam] upon [al] one [sal] basket [ekhad], and you shall bring them near [ve-hiqravta] them [otam] in the basket [ba-sal], and the bull [ve-et ha-par] and the two [u-shnei] rams [ha-eilim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּ אוֹתָם עַל־סַל אֶחָד וְהִקְרַבְתָּ אֹתָם בַּסָּל וְאֶת־הַפָּר וְאֵת שְׁנֵי הָאֵלִים. **Key terms:** סַל (sal) = "basket" — the bread offerings are presented together in a single basket alongside the animals.

**4** And Aaron [ve-et-Aharon] and his sons [ve-et-banav] you shall bring near [taqriv] to [el] the entrance [petakh] of the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed], and you shall wash [ve-rakhatsta] them [otam] with water [ba-mayim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־אַהֲרֹן וְאֶת־בָּנָיו תַּקְרִיב אֶל־פֶּתַח אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד וְרָחַצְתָּ אֹתָם בַּמָּיִם. **Key terms:** וְרָחַצְתָּ (ve-rakhatsta) = qal perfect of רחץ (rakhats, "to wash, bathe") — the first act of consecration is washing; water purification precedes vesting.

**5** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] the garments [et-ha-begadim], and you shall clothe [ve-hilbashta] Aaron [et-Aharon] with the tunic [et-ha-khetonet] and the robe [ve-et me'il] of the ephod [ha-efod] and the ephod [ve-et-ha-efod] and the breastpiece [ve-et-ha-khoshen], and you shall gird [ve-afadeta] him [lo] with the band [be-kheshev] of the ephod [ha-efod].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ אֶת־הַבְּגָדִים וְהִלְבַּשְׁתָּ אֶת־אַהֲרֹן אֶת־הַכֻּתֹּנֶת וְאֵת מְעִיל הָאֵפֹד וְאֶת־הָאֵפֹד וְאֶת־הַחֹשֶׁן וְאָפַדְתָּ לוֹ בְּחֵשֶׁב הָאֵפֹד. **Key terms:** וְאָפַדְתָּ (ve-afadeta) = qal perfect of אפד (afad, "to gird, bind on") — a verb used only for fastening the ephod; denominative from efod.

**6** And you shall set [ve-samta] the turban [ha-mitsnefet] upon [al] his head [rosho], and you shall put [ve-natattah] the crown [et-nezer] of holiness [ha-qodesh] upon [al] the turban [ha-mitsnefet].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשַׂמְתָּ הַמִּצְנֶפֶת עַל־רֹאשׁוֹ וְנָתַתָּ אֶת־נֵזֶר הַקֹּדֶשׁ עַל־הַמִּצְנָפֶת. **Key terms:** נֵזֶר (nezer) = "crown, consecration, diadem" — from נזר (nazar, "to separate, consecrate"); the gold plate (tsits) of 28:36 is here called nezer — it is both a crown and a mark of separation.

**7** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] the oil [et-shemen] of anointing [ha-mishkhah], and you shall pour [ve-yatsaqta] it upon [al] his head [rosho], and you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] him [oto].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ אֶת־שֶׁמֶן הַמִּשְׁחָה וְיָצַקְתָּ עַל־רֹאשׁוֹ וּמָשַׁחְתָּ אֹתוֹ. **Key terms:** שֶׁמֶן הַמִּשְׁחָה (shemen ha-mishkhah) = "oil of anointing" — the special compound described in 30:22-33; pouring upon the head is the definitive act of priestly consecration.

**8** And his sons [ve-et-banav] you shall bring near [taqriv], and you shall clothe [ve-hilbashta] them [otam] with tunics [kuttonot].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־בָּנָיו תַּקְרִיב וְהִלְבַּשְׁתָּם כֻּתֳּנֹת. **Key terms:** כֻּתֳּנֹת (kuttonot) = "tunics" — the ordinary priests receive tunics but not the full array of the high priest's garments.

**9** And you shall gird [ve-khagarta] them [otam] with a sash [avnet] — Aaron [Aharon] and his sons [u-vanav] — and you shall bind [ve-khavashta] headdresses [migba'ot] on them [lahem]; and the priesthood [kehunnat] shall be [ve-hayetah] theirs [lahem] as a statute [le-khuqqat] of perpetuity [olam]; and you shall fill [u-milleita] the hand [yad] of Aaron [Aharon] and the hand [ve-yad] of his sons [banav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְחָגַרְתָּ אֹתָם אַבְנֵט אַהֲרֹן וּבָנָיו וְחָבַשְׁתָּ לָהֶם מִגְבָּעֹת וְהָיְתָה לָהֶם כְּהֻנָּה לְחֻקַּת עוֹלָם וּמִלֵּאתָ יַד־אַהֲרֹן וְיַד־בָּנָיו. **Key terms:** וּמִלֵּאתָ יַד (u-milleita yad) = "and you shall fill the hand" — the idiom for ordination; literally placing the offering portions into the priest's hands, the technical act that constitutes installation.

---

## The Bull of the Sin Offering (vv. 10–14)

**10** And you shall bring near [ve-hiqravta] the bull [et-ha-par] before [lifnei] the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed], and Aaron [ve-samakh Aharon] and his sons [u-vanav] shall lay [et-yedeihem] their hands upon [al] the head [rosh] of the bull [ha-par].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהִקְרַבְתָּ אֶת־הַפָּר לִפְנֵי אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד וְסָמַךְ אַהֲרֹן וּבָנָיו אֶת־יְדֵיהֶם עַל־רֹאשׁ הַפָּר. **Key terms:** וְסָמַךְ (ve-samakh) = qal perfect of סמך (samakh, "to lean, lay") — the hand-laying transfers identity; the offerer identifies with the animal.

**11** And you shall slaughter [ve-shakhatta] the bull [et-ha-par] before [lifnei] YHWH, at the entrance [petakh] of the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשָׁחַטְתָּ אֶת־הַפָּר לִפְנֵי יְהוָה פֶּתַח אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד. **Key terms:** וְשָׁחַטְתָּ (ve-shakhatta) = qal perfect of שׁחט (shakhat, "to slaughter") — the slaughter takes place at the entrance, in YHWH's presence.

**12** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] from the blood [mi-dam] of the bull [ha-par], and you shall put [ve-natattah] it upon [al] the horns [qarnot] of the altar [ha-mizbeakh] with your finger [be-etsba'ekha], and all [ve-et-kol] the blood [ha-dam] you shall pour out [tishpokh] at [el] the base [yesod] of the altar [ha-mizbeakh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ מִדַּם הַפָּר וְנָתַתָּה עַל־קַרְנֹת הַמִּזְבֵּחַ בְּאֶצְבָּעֶךָ וְאֶת־כָּל־הַדָּם תִּשְׁפֹּךְ אֶל־יְסוֹד הַמִּזְבֵּחַ. **Key terms:** בְּאֶצְבָּעֶךָ (be-etsba'ekha) = "with your finger" — the blood application to the altar horns is manual, not by sprinkling; an intimate, deliberate act.

**13** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] all [et-kol] the fat [ha-khelev] covering [ha-mekhasseh] the innards [et-ha-qerev], and the appendage [ve-et ha-yoteret] upon [al] the liver [ha-kaved], and the two [ve-et shtei] kidneys [ha-kelayot] and the fat [ve-et-ha-khelev] which [asher] is upon them [aleihen], and you shall turn them to smoke [ve-hiqtarta] on the altar [ha-mizbkhah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ אֶת־כָּל־הַחֵלֶב הַמְכַסֶּה אֶת־הַקֶּרֶב וְאֵת הַיֹּתֶרֶת עַל־הַכָּבֵד וְאֵת שְׁתֵּי הַכְּלָיֹת וְאֶת־הַחֵלֶב אֲשֶׁר עֲלֵיהֶן וְהִקְטַרְתָּ הַמִּזְבֵּחָה. **Key terms:** וְהִקְטַרְתָּ (ve-hiqtarta) = hiphil perfect of קטר (qatar, "to turn to smoke, make sacrificial smoke") — not "burn" but "cause to become fragrant smoke"; the fat ascends as smoke to YHWH.

**14** And the flesh [u-vesar] of the bull [ha-par] and its skin [ve-oro] and its dung [u-firsho] you shall burn [tisrof] with fire [ba-esh] outside [mi-khuts] the camp [la-makhaneh]; it [khattat] is a sin offering [hu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְשַׂר הַפָּר וְאֶת־עֹרוֹ וְאֶת־פִּרְשׁוֹ תִּשְׂרֹף בָּאֵשׁ מִחוּץ לַמַּחֲנֶה חַטָּאת הוּא. **Key terms:** חַטָּאת (khattat) = "sin offering" — from חטא (khata, "to miss, sin"); the bull's flesh is not eaten but burned entirely outside the camp, distinguishing the sin offering from other sacrifices.

---

## The First Ram — Burnt Offering (vv. 15–18)

**15** And the one [ha-ayil ha-ekhad] ram [tiqakh] you shall take, and Aaron [ve-samakhu Aharon] and his sons [u-vanav] shall lay their hands [et-yedeihem] upon [al] the head [rosh] of the ram [ha-ayil].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־הָאַיִל הָאֶחָד תִּקָּח וְסָמְכוּ אַהֲרֹן וּבָנָיו אֶת־יְדֵיהֶם עַל־רֹאשׁ הָאָיִל. **Key terms:** הָאַיִל (ha-ayil) = "the ram" — from אול (ul, "to be strong"); the first of two rams, each serving a distinct sacrificial function.

**16** And you shall slaughter [ve-shakhatta] the ram [et-ha-ayil], and you shall take [ve-laqakhta] its blood [et-damo], and you shall throw [ve-zaraqta] it upon [al] the altar [ha-mizbeakh] all around [saviv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשָׁחַטְתָּ אֶת־הָאָיִל וְלָקַחְתָּ אֶת־דָּמוֹ וְזָרַקְתָּ עַל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ סָבִיב. **Key terms:** וְזָרַקְתָּ (ve-zaraqta) = qal perfect of זרק (zaraq, "to throw, dash, toss") — the blood is thrown against the altar sides, not dabbed with the finger as with the sin offering.

**17** And the ram [ve-et-ha-ayil] you shall cut [tenattakh] into its pieces [li-ntakhav], and you shall wash [ve-rakhatsta] its innards [et-qirbo] and its legs [ve-et-kera'av], and you shall put [ve-natattah] them upon [al] its pieces [netakhav] and upon [ve-al] its head [rosho].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־הָאַיִל תְּנַתֵּחַ לִנְתָחָיו וְרָחַצְתָּ אֶת־קִרְבּוֹ וְאֶת־כְּרָעָיו וְנָתַתָּ עַל־נְתָחָיו וְעַל־רֹאשׁוֹ. **Key terms:** תְּנַתֵּחַ (tenattakh) = piel imperfect of נתח (natakh, "to cut in pieces") — ritual dismemberment for orderly arrangement on the altar.

**18** And you shall turn to smoke [ve-hiqtarta] all [et-kol] the ram [ha-ayil] on the altar [ha-mizbkhah]; it is a burnt offering [olah] to YHWH [hu la-YHWH], a pleasing [nikhokh] aroma [reakh]; it is an offering by fire [ishsheh] to YHWH [hu la-YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהִקְטַרְתָּ אֶת־כָּל־הָאַיִל הַמִּזְבֵּחָה עֹלָה הוּא לַיהוָה רֵיחַ נִיחוֹחַ אִשֶּׁה הוּא לַיהוָה. **Key terms:** רֵיחַ נִיחוֹחַ (reakh nikhokh) = "a pleasing aroma" — literally "an aroma of soothing/rest"; from נוח (nuakh, "to rest"); the ascending smoke brings YHWH satisfaction. עֹלָה (olah) = "burnt offering" — from עלה (alah, "to go up"); the entire animal ascends as smoke.

---

## The Ram of Ordination (vv. 19–28)

**19** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] the second [ha-sheni] ram [et-ha-ayil], and Aaron [ve-samakh Aharon] and his sons [u-vanav] shall lay their hands [et-yedeihem] upon [al] the head [rosh] of the ram [ha-ayil].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ אֵת הָאַיִל הַשֵּׁנִי וְסָמַךְ אַהֲרֹן וּבָנָיו אֶת־יְדֵיהֶם עַל־רֹאשׁ הָאָיִל. The second ram is the distinctive "ram of ordination" (eil millu'im, v. 22).

**20** And you shall slaughter [ve-shakhatta] the ram [et-ha-ayil], and you shall take [ve-laqakhta] from its blood [mi-damo], and you shall put [ve-natattah] it upon [al] the lobe [tenukh] of the ear [ozen] of Aaron [Aharon] and upon [ve-al] the lobe [tenukh] of the ear [ozen] of his sons [banav] — the right one [ha-yemanit] — and upon [ve-al] the thumb [bohen] of their right hand [yadam ha-yemanit] and upon [ve-al] the great toe [bohen] of their right foot [raglam ha-yemanit]; and you shall throw [ve-zaraqta] the blood [et-ha-dam] upon [al] the altar [ha-mizbeakh] all around [saviv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשָׁחַטְתָּ אֶת־הָאָיִל וְלָקַחְתָּ מִדָּמוֹ וְנָתַתָּה עַל־תְּנוּךְ אֹזֶן אַהֲרֹן וְעַל־תְּנוּךְ אֹזֶן בָּנָיו הַיְמָנִית וְעַל־בֹּהֶן יָדָם הַיְמָנִית וְעַל־בֹּהֶן רַגְלָם הַיְמָנִית וְזָרַקְתָּ אֶת־הַדָּם עַל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ סָבִיב. **Key terms:** תְּנוּךְ (tenukh) = "lobe, tip" — the extremities of the body (ear, thumb, toe) are consecrated; the priest hears, acts, and walks in holiness.

**21** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] from the blood [min-ha-dam] which [asher] is upon [al] the altar [ha-mizbeakh] and from the oil [u-mi-shemen] of anointing [ha-mishkhah], and you shall sprinkle [ve-hizzita] upon [al] Aaron [Aharon] and upon [ve-al] his garments [begadav], and upon [ve-al] his sons [banav] and upon [ve-al] the garments [bigdei] of his sons [banav] with him [itto]; and he shall be holy [ve-qadash] — he [hu] and his garments [u-vegadav] and his sons [u-vanav] and the garments [u-vigdei] of his sons [vanav] with him [itto].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ מִן־הַדָּם אֲשֶׁר עַל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ וּמִשֶּׁמֶן הַמִּשְׁחָה וְהִזֵּיתָ עַל־אַהֲרֹן וְעַל־בְּגָדָיו וְעַל־בָּנָיו וְעַל־בִּגְדֵי בָנָיו אִתּוֹ וְקָדַשׁ הוּא וּבְגָדָיו וּבָנָיו וּבִגְדֵי בָנָיו אִתּוֹ. **Key terms:** וְהִזֵּיתָ (ve-hizzita) = hiphil perfect of נזה (nazah, "to sprinkle") — blood and oil mingled together sprinkled upon persons and garments; the consecration permeates both the priest and his vestments.

**22** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] from the ram [min-ha-ayil] the fat [ha-khelev] and the fat tail [ve-ha-alyah] and the fat [ve-et-ha-khelev] covering [ha-mekhasseh] the innards [et-ha-qerev], and the appendage [ve-et yoteret] of the liver [ha-kaved], and the two [ve-et shtei] kidneys [ha-kelayot] and the fat [ve-et-ha-khelev] which [asher] is upon them [aleihen], and the right [ha-yamin] thigh [ve-et shoq] — for it [ki] is a ram [eil] of ordination [millu'im] it is [hu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ מִן־הָאַיִל הַחֵלֶב וְהָאַלְיָה וְאֶת־הַחֵלֶב הַמְכַסֶּה אֶת־הַקֶּרֶב וְאֵת יֹתֶרֶת הַכָּבֵד וְאֵת שְׁתֵּי הַכְּלָיֹת וְאֶת־הַחֵלֶב אֲשֶׁר עֲלֵיהֶן וְאֵת שׁוֹק הַיָּמִין כִּי אֵיל מִלֻּאִים הוּא. **Key terms:** אֵיל מִלֻּאִים (eil millu'im) = "ram of ordination/fillings" — from מלא (male, "to fill"); the ram whose portions will "fill the hands" of the priests.

**23** And one [ve-kikkar] loaf [lekhem] of bread [ekhat] and one cake [ve-khallat lekhem shemen akhat] of oiled bread and one wafer [ve-raqiq ekhad] from the basket [mi-sal] of the unleavened bread [ha-matstsot] which [asher] is before [lifnei] YHWH.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְכִכַּר לֶחֶם אַחַת וְחַלַּת לֶחֶם שֶׁמֶן אַחַת וְרָקִיק אֶחָד מִסַּל הַמַּצּוֹת אֲשֶׁר לִפְנֵי יְהוָה. **Key terms:** כִּכָּר (kikkar) = "loaf, round" — from כרר (karar, "to be round"); a round loaf; one of each bread type from v. 2 is selected.

**24** And you shall set [ve-samta] all [ha-kol] upon [al] the palms [kappei] of Aaron [Aharon] and upon [ve-al] the palms [kappei] of his sons [banav], and you shall wave [ve-henafta] them [otam] as a wave offering [tenufah] before [lifnei] YHWH.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשַׂמְתָּ הַכֹּל עַל כַּפֵּי אַהֲרֹן וְעַל כַּפֵּי בָנָיו וְהֵנַפְתָּ אֹתָם תְּנוּפָה לִפְנֵי יְהוָה. **Key terms:** תְּנוּפָה (tenufah) = "wave offering" — from נוף (nuf, "to wave, swing"); the priest's filled hands are waved before YHWH — this is the literal "hand-filling" that constitutes ordination.

**25** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] them [otam] from their hands [mi-yadam], and you shall turn them to smoke [ve-hiqtarta] on the altar [ha-mizbkhah], upon [al] the burnt offering [ha-olah], as a pleasing [nikhokh] aroma [le-reakh] before [lifnei] YHWH; it is an offering by fire [ishsheh] to YHWH [hu la-YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ אֹתָם מִיָּדָם וְהִקְטַרְתָּ הַמִּזְבֵּחָה עַל־הָעֹלָה לְרֵיחַ נִיחֹחַ לִפְנֵי יְהוָה אִשֶּׁה הוּא לַיהוָה. The fat portions and bread are burned on top of the burnt offering already on the altar.

**26** And you shall take [ve-laqakhta] the breast [et-he-khazeh] from the ram [me-eil] of ordination [ha-millu'im] which [asher] is for Aaron [le-Aharon], and you shall wave it [ve-henafta] as a wave offering [oto tenufah] before [lifnei] YHWH; and it shall be [ve-hayah] your portion [le-kha le-manah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלָקַחְתָּ אֶת־הֶחָזֶה מֵאֵיל הַמִּלֻּאִים אֲשֶׁר לְאַהֲרֹן וְהֵנַפְתָּ אֹתוֹ תְּנוּפָה לִפְנֵי יְהוָה וְהָיָה לְךָ לְמָנָה. **Key terms:** חָזֶה (khazeh) = "breast" — the breast portion goes to Moses as the officiant of this unique ceremony.

**27** And you shall consecrate [ve-qiddashta] the breast [et-khazeh] of the wave offering [ha-tenufah] and the thigh [ve-et shoq] of the contribution [ha-terumah] which [asher] was waved [hunaf] and which [va-asher] was raised [huram], from the ram [me-eil] of ordination [ha-millu'im] — from that [me-asher] which is for Aaron [le-Aharon] and from that [u-me-asher] which is for his sons [le-vanav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְקִדַּשְׁתָּ אֶת חֲזֵה הַתְּנוּפָה וְאֵת שׁוֹק הַתְּרוּמָה אֲשֶׁר הוּנַף וַאֲשֶׁר הוּרָם מֵאֵיל הַמִּלֻּאִים מֵאֲשֶׁר לְאַהֲרֹן וּמֵאֲשֶׁר לְבָנָיו. **Key terms:** תְּרוּמָה (terumah) = "contribution, raised offering" — from רום (rum, "to be high, to raise"); the thigh is "raised up" as YHWH's portion. The distinction between tenufah (horizontal waving) and terumah (vertical raising) establishes two modes of presenting offerings.

**28** And it shall be [ve-hayah] for Aaron [le-Aharon] and for his sons [u-le-vanav] as a statute [le-khoq] of perpetuity [olam] from [me-et] the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael], for it is [ki] a contribution [terumah] it is [hu]; and a contribution [u-terumah] it shall be [yihyeh] from [me-et] the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael] from their sacrifices [mi-zivkhei] of peace offerings [shalmeihem] — their contribution [terumatam] to YHWH [la-YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיָה לְאַהֲרֹן וּלְבָנָיו לְחָק־עוֹלָם מֵאֵת בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל כִּי תְרוּמָה הוּא וּתְרוּמָה יִהְיֶה מֵאֵת בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל מִזִּבְחֵי שַׁלְמֵיהֶם תְּרוּמָתָם לַיהוָה. **Key terms:** שְׁלָמִים (shelamim) = "peace offerings" — from שׁלם (shalam, "to be complete, whole"); the only sacrifice where the offerer, priest, and YHWH all share in the meal.

---

## The Succession of Garments (vv. 29–30)

**29** And the garments [u-vigdei] of holiness [ha-qodesh] which [asher] are for Aaron [le-Aharon] shall be [yihyu] for his sons [le-vanav] after him [akharav], to be anointed [le-moshkhah] in them [bahem] and to fill [u-le-mallei] their hand [et-yadam] in them [bahem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבִגְדֵי הַקֹּדֶשׁ אֲשֶׁר לְאַהֲרֹן יִהְיוּ לְבָנָיו אַחֲרָיו לְמָשְׁחָה בָהֶם וּלְמַלֵּא בָם אֶת־יָדָם. **Key terms:** לְמָשְׁחָה (le-moshkhah) = "for anointing" — the garments are inherited and used in each successive ordination; the priesthood is dynastic.

**30** Seven [shiv'at] days [yamim] the one who is priest [ha-kohen] after him [takhtav] from his sons [mi-banav] shall wear them [yilbasham], who [asher] comes [yavo] into [el] the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed] to minister [le-sharet] in the holy place [ba-qodesh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שִׁבְעַת יָמִים יִלְבָּשָׁם הַכֹּהֵן תַּחְתָּיו מִבָּנָיו אֲשֶׁר יָבֹא אֶל־אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד לְשָׁרֵת בַּקֹּדֶשׁ. **Key terms:** תַּחְתָּיו (takhtav) = "in his place, after him" — from תחת (takhat, "under, instead of"); succession is spatial — the next priest stands "under" or "in the place of" the former.

---

## The Ordination Meal (vv. 31–34)

**31** And the ram [ve-et eil] of ordination [ha-millu'im] you shall take [tiqakh], and you shall boil [u-vishshalta] its flesh [et-besaro] in a holy [qadosh] place [be-maqom].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֵת אֵיל הַמִּלֻּאִים תִּקָּח וּבִשַּׁלְתָּ אֶת־בְּשָׂרוֹ בְּמָקוֹם קָדֹשׁ. **Key terms:** וּבִשַּׁלְתָּ (u-vishshalta) = piel perfect of בשׁל (bashal, "to boil, cook") — the ordination ram's flesh is cooked, not burned; it will be eaten as a consecration meal.

**32** And Aaron [ve-akhal Aharon] and his sons [u-vanav] shall eat the flesh [et-besar] of the ram [ha-ayil] and the bread [ve-et-ha-lekhem] which [asher] is in the basket [ba-sal], at the entrance [petakh] of the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאָכַל אַהֲרֹן וּבָנָיו אֶת־בְּשַׂר הָאַיִל וְאֶת־הַלֶּחֶם אֲשֶׁר בַּסָּל פֶּתַח אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד. **Key terms:** וְאָכַל (ve-akhal) = qal perfect of אכל (akhal, "to eat") — the priests eat the sacred meal at the entrance of YHWH's dwelling; eating in God's presence is the culmination of ordination.

**33** And they shall eat [ve-akhelu] them [otam] — those things by which [asher] atonement was made [kuppar] in them [bahem] to fill [le-mallei] their hand [et-yadam], to consecrate [le-qaddesh] them [otam]; and a stranger [ve-zar] shall not eat [lo yokhal], for they [ki] are holy [qodesh] they are [hem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאָכְלוּ אֹתָם אֲשֶׁר כֻּפַּר בָּהֶם לְמַלֵּא אֶת־יָדָם לְקַדֵּשׁ אֹתָם וְזָר לֹא־יֹאכַל כִּי־קֹדֶשׁ הֵם. **Key terms:** כֻּפַּר (kuppar) = pual perfect of כפר (kafar, "to cover, atone") — "atonement was made"; the ordination offerings effect atonement. וְזָר (ve-zar) = "and a stranger/outsider" — anyone not of the priestly line; the sacred meal is restricted.

**34** And if [ve-im] there remains [yivvater] from the flesh [mi-besar] of ordination [ha-millu'im] and from [u-min] the bread [ha-lekhem] until [ad] the morning [ha-boqer], then you shall burn [ve-sarafta] the remainder [et-ha-notar] with fire [ba-esh]; it shall not be eaten [lo ye'akhel], for it is [ki] holy [qodesh] it is [hu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאִם־יִוָּתֵר מִבְּשַׂר הַמִּלֻּאִים וּמִן־הַלֶּחֶם עַד־הַבֹּקֶר וְשָׂרַפְתָּ אֶת־הַנּוֹתָר בָּאֵשׁ לֹא יֵאָכֵל כִּי־קֹדֶשׁ הוּא. **Key terms:** יִוָּתֵר (yivvater) = niphal imperfect of יתר (yatar, "to remain, be left over") — sacred food cannot be stored; holiness has a temporal boundary.

---

## Seven Days of Consecration (vv. 35–37)

**35** And you shall do [ve-asita] thus [kakhah] to Aaron [le-Aharon] and to his sons [u-le-vanav] according to all [ke-khol] that [asher] I have commanded [tsivviti] you [otakh]; seven [shiv'at] days [yamim] you shall fill [temallei] their hand [yadam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ לְאַהֲרֹן וּלְבָנָיו כָּכָה כְּכֹל אֲשֶׁר־צִוִּיתִי אֹתָכָה שִׁבְעַת יָמִים תְּמַלֵּא יָדָם. **Key terms:** שִׁבְעַת יָמִים (shiv'at yamim) = "seven days" — the full cycle of creation; consecration requires a complete week, mirroring YHWH's own seven-day creative act.

**36** And a bull [u-far] of sin offering [khattat] you shall make [ta'aseh] each day [la-yom] upon [al] the atonements [ha-kippurim]; and you shall purify [ve-khitteta] the altar [al-ha-mizbeakh] by your making atonement [be-khaperakh] upon it [alav], and you shall anoint [u-mashakhta] it [oto] to consecrate it [le-qadesho].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּפַר חַטָּאת תַּעֲשֶׂה לַיּוֹם עַל־הַכִּפֻּרִים וְחִטֵּאתָ עַל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ בְּכַפֶּרְךָ עָלָיו וּמָשַׁחְתָּ אֹתוֹ לְקַדְּשׁוֹ. **Key terms:** וְחִטֵּאתָ (ve-khitteta) = piel perfect of חטא (khata) — "you shall de-sin, purify"; the piel of חטא reverses the qal meaning, turning "to sin" into "to purify from sin."

**37** Seven [shiv'at] days [yamim] you shall make atonement [tekapper] upon [al] the altar [ha-mizbeakh] and you shall consecrate [ve-qiddashta] it [oto]; and the altar [ve-hayah ha-mizbeakh] shall be [qodesh] most holy [qodashim]; all [kol] that touches [ha-nogea] the altar [ba-mizbeakh] shall be holy [yiqdash].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שִׁבְעַת יָמִים תְּכַפֵּר עַל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ וְקִדַּשְׁתָּ אֹתוֹ וְהָיָה הַמִּזְבֵּחַ קֹדֶשׁ קָדָשִׁים כָּל־הַנֹּגֵעַ בַּמִּזְבֵּחַ יִקְדָּשׁ. **Key terms:** קֹדֶשׁ קָדָשִׁים (qodesh qodashim) = "holy of holies, most holy" — the superlative construction; the altar becomes so saturated with holiness that contact with it transmits holiness.

---

## The Daily Offerings — Tamid (vv. 38–42)

**38** And this [ve-zeh] is what [asher] you shall offer [ta'aseh] upon [al] the altar [ha-mizbeakh]: lambs [kevasim] — sons [benei] of a year [shanah] — two [shnayim] each day [la-yom], continually [tamid].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְזֶה אֲשֶׁר תַּעֲשֶׂה עַל־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ כְּבָשִׂים בְּנֵי־שָׁנָה שְׁנַיִם לַיּוֹם תָּמִיד. **Key terms:** תָּמִיד (tamid) = "continually, perpetually" — the tamid offering defines the rhythm of Israel's worship; two lambs per day, every day, without ceasing.

**39** The one [ha-ekhad] lamb [et-ha-keves] you shall offer [ta'aseh] in the morning [ba-boqer], and the second [ha-sheni] lamb [ve-et ha-keves] you shall offer [ta'aseh] between [bein] the evenings [ha-arbayim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אֶת־הַכֶּבֶשׂ הָאֶחָד תַּעֲשֶׂה בַבֹּקֶר וְאֵת הַכֶּבֶשׂ הַשֵּׁנִי תַּעֲשֶׂה בֵּין הָעַרְבָּיִם. **Key terms:** בֵּין הָעַרְבָּיִם (bein ha-arbayim) = "between the evenings" — literally "between the two settings"; traditionally understood as late afternoon (approximately 3:00-5:00 PM).

**40** And a tenth [ve-issaron] of fine flour [solet] mixed [balul] with oil [be-shemen] — beaten [katit] — a fourth [revi'it] of a hin [ha-hin]; and a drink offering [ve-nesekh] — a fourth [revi'it] of a hin [ha-hin] of wine [yayin] — for the one [la-keves] lamb [ha-ekhad].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעִשָּׂרֹן סֹלֶת בָּלוּל בְּשֶׁמֶן כָּתִית רְבִעִית הַהִין וְנֵסֶךְ רְבִיעִת הַהִין יָיִן לַכֶּבֶשׂ הָאֶחָד. **Key terms:** כָּתִית (katit) = "beaten, crushed" — from כתת (katat, "to crush"); the finest quality oil, produced by crushing rather than pressing. הִין (hin) = a liquid measure, approximately 6 liters; a quarter-hin is about 1.5 liters.

**41** And the second [ha-sheni] lamb [ve-et ha-keves] you shall offer [ta'aseh] between [bein] the evenings [ha-arbayim]; like the grain offering [ke-minkhat] of the morning [ha-boqer] and like its drink offering [u-khe-niskah] you shall offer [ta'aseh] for it [lah], as a pleasing [nikhokh] aroma [le-reakh], an offering by fire [ishsheh] to YHWH [la-YHWH].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֵת הַכֶּבֶשׂ הַשֵּׁנִי תַּעֲשֶׂה בֵּין הָעַרְבָּיִם כְּמִנְחַת הַבֹּקֶר וּכְנִסְכָּהּ תַּעֲשֶׂה־לָּהּ לְרֵיחַ נִיחֹחַ אִשֶּׁה לַיהוָה. **Key terms:** מִנְחָה (minkhah) = "grain offering, tribute" — from a root meaning "to give a gift"; the accompanying flour-and-oil offering that complements the animal sacrifice.

**42** A burnt offering [olat] of continual regularity [tamid] throughout your generations [le-doroteikhem], at the entrance [petakh] of the tent [ohel] of meeting [mo'ed] before [lifnei] YHWH, where [asher] I will meet [ivvad] with you [lakhem] there [shammah], to speak [le-dabber] to you [eilekha] there [sham].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads עֹלַת תָּמִיד לְדֹרֹתֵיכֶם פֶּתַח אֹהֶל־מוֹעֵד לִפְנֵי יְהוָה אֲשֶׁר אִוָּעֵד לָכֶם שָׁמָּה לְדַבֵּר אֵלֶיךָ שָׁם. **Key terms:** אִוָּעֵד (ivvad) = niphal imperfect of יעד (ya'ad, "to appoint, meet by appointment") — "I will meet by appointment"; YHWH does not encounter Israel randomly but at a designated place and time. The tamid offering creates the occasion for divine-human meeting.

---

## The Divine Promise of Dwelling (vv. 43–46)

**43** And I will meet there [ve-no'adti shammah] with the sons of Israel [li-vnei Yisrael], and it shall be consecrated [ve-niqdash] by my glory [bi-khevodi].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנֹעַדְתִּי שָׁמָּה לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְנִקְדַּשׁ בִּכְבֹדִי. **Key terms:** וְנִקְדַּשׁ (ve-niqdash) = niphal perfect of קדשׁ — "and it shall be consecrated"; the tabernacle is made holy not by human ritual alone but by the weight of YHWH's kavod ("glory") filling it.

**44** And I will consecrate [ve-qiddashti] the tent [et-ohel] of meeting [mo'ed] and the altar [ve-et-ha-mizbeakh]; and Aaron [ve-et-Aharon] and his sons [ve-et-banav] I will consecrate [aqaddesh] to be priests [le-khahen] to me [li].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְקִדַּשְׁתִּי אֶת־אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד וְאֶת־הַמִּזְבֵּחַ וְאֶת־אַהֲרֹן וְאֶת־בָּנָיו אֲקַדֵּשׁ לְכַהֵן לִי. **Key terms:** אֲקַדֵּשׁ (aqaddesh) = piel imperfect 1cs — "I will consecrate"; YHWH Himself is the ultimate consecrator; the elaborate human rituals of vv. 1-37 find their efficacy in divine action.

**45** And I will dwell [ve-shakhanti] in the midst [be-tokh] of the sons of Israel [benei Yisrael], and I will be [ve-hayiti] their God [lahem le-Elohim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשָׁכַנְתִּי בְּתוֹךְ בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְהָיִיתִי לָהֶם לֵאלֹהִים. **Key terms:** וְשָׁכַנְתִּי (ve-shakhanti) = qal perfect of שׁכן (shakhan, "to dwell, tabernacle") — the root of mishkan ("tabernacle"); YHWH's dwelling among Israel is the telos of the entire tabernacle project. The entire construction sequence (chapters 25-29) drives toward this single declaration.

**46** And they shall know [ve-yad'u] that [ki] I [ani] am YHWH their God [Eloheihem] who [asher] brought them out [hotseti] them [otam] from the land [me-erets] of Egypt [Mitsrayim] to dwell [le-shokhni] in their midst [be-tokham]; I [ani] am YHWH their God [Eloheihem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְיָדְעוּ כִּי אֲנִי יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵיהֶם אֲשֶׁר הוֹצֵאתִי אֹתָם מֵאֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם לְשָׁכְנִי בְתוֹכָם אֲנִי יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵיהֶם. **Key terms:** לְשָׁכְנִי (le-shokhni) = qal infinitive construct of שׁכן + 1cs suffix — "to dwell, my dwelling"; the Exodus was not ultimately for Israel's freedom but for YHWH's habitation; the purpose of liberation is divine presence.

---

## Translator's Afterword

Exodus 29 is the longest sustained ritual text in the Torah's tabernacle instructions, and its forty-six verses answer a single question: how does a mortal become fit to stand in the presence of the holy God? The answer unfolds in layers of blood, oil, fire, and time.

The chapter divides into two major movements. The first (vv. 1-37) describes the ordination ceremony itself — a seven-day process involving three distinct animal sacrifices, each with its own theology. The bull sin offering (vv. 10-14) addresses the priests' guilt; its flesh is burned outside the camp because it absorbs impurity. The first ram as burnt offering (vv. 15-18) ascends entirely to YHWH as reakh nikhokh ("a pleasing aroma") — complete surrender. The second ram, the eil millu'im ("ram of ordination," vv. 19-28), is unique to this ceremony: its blood is applied to the priest's ear, thumb, and toe — consecrating hearing, action, and movement — and its portions fill the priest's hands in the literal gesture that gives ordination its name (millu'im, "fillings").

The second movement (vv. 38-46) pivots from the singular event of ordination to the perpetual rhythm of daily worship. The tamid ("continual") offerings — two lambs per day, morning and evening — establish the ongoing pulse of Israel's relationship with YHWH. The chapter then climaxes in vv. 43-46 with YHWH's declaration of intent: ve-shakhanti be-tokh benei Yisrael ("and I will dwell in the midst of the sons of Israel"). This single statement is the telos of everything since Exodus 25:1. The tabernacle, the furniture, the garments, the consecration — all serve one purpose: creating the conditions for YHWH's habitation among His people. The final verse reframes the Exodus itself: YHWH brought Israel out of Egypt le-shokhni be-tokham ("to dwell in their midst") — liberation was never the end but the means; divine presence is the goal.

Key MT-LXX divergences: (1) The Vaticanus text follows the MT sequence closely in this chapter, with fewer structural rearrangements than in chapter 28. (2) The LXX consistently renders YHWH as κύριος ("Lord"). (3) The LXX translates eil millu'im as κριὸν τῆς τελειώσεως ("ram of completion/perfection"), interpreting millu'im through the lens of τελείωσις — the same root used in Hebrews for Christ's priestly "perfecting." (4) In v. 46, the LXX renders le-shokhni be-tokham as ἐπικληθῆναι αὐτοῖς ("to be called upon by them") in some witnesses, though Vaticanus preserves the dwelling language. (5) The blood-on-the-ear-thumb-toe ritual (v. 20) is rendered straightforwardly in the LXX without expansion. (6) The LXX renders khattat (v. 14) as περὶ ἁμαρτίας ("concerning sin") rather than a single technical term, revealing the interpretive nature of the Greek translation.
